events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess field condition: condensate line without trap or sloped wrong for furnace & venting. Work rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Condensate line missing trap or wrong slope is non-compliant and installed-defective. It can cause flue gases escape, furnace pressure switch issues, water damage. Risk maybe medium/high. Need references: IRC M1801? Actually condensate drainage maybe IRC M1801 covers furnace installation? NFPA 54 venting. Manufacturer requires trap and proper slope. Decision fail. Need include findings and actions. Need fill all fields. percent_complete maybe 80. value 0. sound_source empty? Schema string; can use "". confidence 0.95. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty list. workable empty list. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Let's craft JSON. Ensure no prose outside JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],

  "findings": [
    "Condensate drain line is missing a required trap.",
    "Condensate line is sloped incorrectly, preventing proper drainage.",
    "Condition can allow flue gases to escape through the drain opening and cause pressure-switch or corrosion issues."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a properly sized condensate trap per OEM instructions.",
    "Re-route and slope the condensate line toward the drain at least 1/4 inch per foot.",
    "Verify furnace pressure switch operation and check for flue-gas spillage after correction."
  ],
  "rationale": "A condensate line without a trap or with incorrect slope violates manufacturer installation requirements and venting/safety provisions; it is a non-compliant, defective installation that must be corrected before close-out.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54",
    "OEM furnace installation instructions"
  ]
}
